title: Add spec.resources field to Keystone CRD
summary: Adds an optional spec.resources field (*corev1.ResourceRequirements) to KeystoneSpec with webhook defaulting (256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU), validation (request must not exceed limit), deployment wiring via containerResources() helper, CRD regeneration + Helm sync, integration tests, and CRD reference documentation. Implementation is clean, follows existing patterns, and all tests pass. Two test coverage gaps need addressing before approval.

tests_checklist.item: go vet passes with no errors
Webhook defaulting tests cover nil→defaults and explicit preserved
Webhook validation tests cover valid, CPU exceeds, memory exceeds, both exceed, nil, requests-only
Deployment builder tests cover default resources and custom resources
Integration tests cover defaulting, preservation, and rejection
TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults updated to assert Resources non-nil
TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations updated to include resources violation
containerResources() nil path has dedicated test

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing webhook defaulting pattern (nil-check → assign)
Uses specPath.Child() for field paths consistently
containerResources() helper is nil-safe with defense-in-depth comment
DeepCopy regenerated correctly for pointer field
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: All user inputs validated (resource requests vs limits)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — one field, one defaulting block, one validation block, one helper
Field placement in KeystoneSpec follows existing ordering convention (after NetworkPolicy, before ExtraConfig)
CRD manifest regenerated and synced to Helm chart
No new RBAC markers needed (resources wired into existing deployment)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No over-engineering — containerResources() is the minimum necessary abstraction
No unused imports or dead code

fail_fast_checklist.item: Validation errors accumulated into field.ErrorList (no short-circuit)
Nil-safety in containerResources() prevents panic on webhook-bypass CRs

defensive_checklist.item: Webhook defaulting guards with nil check
Validation guards with Resources != nil && Limits != nil before iterating
containerResources() returns zero-value on nil (defense-in-depth for webhook bypass)

issues_found.description: TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults (line 46-59) does not assert that Resources is non-nil with correct defaults after defaulting. Task 2.1 item 5 explicitly required: 'Update TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults to also assert Resources is non-nil with defaults.' This means the comprehensive zero-value defaults test does not verify the new Resources defaulting, breaking the contract that this test covers ALL defaulted fields.
issues_found.fix: Add assertions after line 58: g.Expect(k.Spec.Resources).NotTo(BeNil()), g.Expect(k.Spec.Resources.Requests).To(HaveKeyWithValue(corev1.ResourceMemory, resource.MustParse("256Mi"))), and equivalent for CPU, Limits.Memory, Limits.CPU.

issues_found.description: TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations (line 876-920) claims to exercise ALL validation paths simultaneously to prove error accumulation (no short-circuiting), but does not include a broken spec.resources entry. Per external review pattern 'ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-their-coverage-scope': the test name contains a universal quantifier ('AllValidations') but the new resources validation path is not exercised. This gives false confidence that the test proves all errors are accumulated.
issues_found.fix: Add resources violation after the networkPolicy setup (around line 905): k.Spec.Resources = &corev1.ResourceRequirements{Requests: corev1.ResourceList{corev1.ResourceCPU: resource.MustParse("1000m")}, Limits: corev1.ResourceList{corev1.ResourceCPU: resource.MustParse("500m")}}. Add assertion: g.Expect(errMsg).To(ContainSubstring("resources")).

issues_found.description: No test covers containerResources() with nil spec.Resources input. The function has an explicit nil-safety fallback (return corev1.ResourceRequirements{}) for CRs that bypass the webhook, but this defense-in-depth path is untested. If the nil check were accidentally removed, no test would catch the resulting nil-pointer panic.
issues_found.fix: Add TestReconcileDeployment_NilResources: create a Keystone via deployTestKeystone(), set ks.Spec.Resources = nil, call buildKeystoneDeployment(), assert container.Resources equals corev1.ResourceRequirements{} (zero value with empty Requests and Limits).

suggested_improvements: Consider including the limit value in the validation error message for better operator UX: fmt.Sprintf("%s request must not exceed limit (%s)", resourceName, limit.String()) — the plan specified this format and it gives operators the limit value without requiring them to look at the CR spec.

The networkPolicy row added to the KeystoneSpec docs table (line 119) appears to be a documentation gap fix from CC-0039, not part of CC-0042 scope. Consider splitting this into a separate commit for clean traceability, or add a comment noting it fills a CC-0039 documentation gap.
next_steps: Fix MAJOR: Add Resources assertions to TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults
Fix MAJOR: Add resources violation to TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations with assertion for 'resources' in error message
Fix MAJOR: Add TestReconcileDeployment_NilResources for the nil-safety fallback path
Re-run go test ./operators/keystone/... to confirm all tests pass after fixes
